topics: Basics of Biomedical Signal Processing
Introduction and Relevance of Biomedical Signal Processing
Biomedical Signals - ECG, Carotid Pulse, Speech Signal, and Concurrent Signals
Signal Representation and Aliasing - Sampling Theorem
Elementary Signals and Classification of Discrete Signals - Energy and Power Signals, Periodic and Aperiodic, Even and Odd Signals
Discrete Time Systems - Properties, LTI System, Impulse Response, Convolution, Correlation, and Difference Equation Representation
Fourier Analysis for Continuous Time Processes - Synthesis of an ECG Signal Using Pure Sinusoid
Frequency Domain Analysis of Discrete Time Signals - Discrete Fourier Series, Discrete Time Fourier Transform, and Discrete Fourier Transform Properties
Fast Fourier Transform - FFT Algorithms (Radix 2) - Decimation in Time and Decimation in Frequency
Spectrum Analysis of Bio Signals - Introduction to Parametric and Non-Parametric Methods
Z Transform - Region of Convergence (ROC) and Properties of ROC
Properties of Z Transform - Linearity, Time Shifting, Scaling, Time Reversal, Differentiation in Z Domain, Convolution, Correlation, Multiplication, Parseval's Relation, and Initial Value Theorem
Rational Z Transform - Poles and Zeros, LTI System Function, and Inverse Z Transform (Long Division, Partial Fraction Expansion, Residue, and Convolution Methods)
Digital Filters - Concept, Types, and Comparison with Analog Filters
FIR Filter Design Using Fourier Series and Window Functions

IIR Digital Filters - Analog Filter Approximations: Butterworth Approximation, Frequency Transformation Techniques, Impulse Invariant Transformation, Matched Z Transform Technique, and Bilinear Transformation
Project Areas in Biomedical Signal Processing
Filtering for Removal of Artefacts - Implementation of Time Domain and Frequency Domain Filters for Removal of High Frequency, Low Frequency, and Periodic Artefacts
Event Detection in Biomedical Signals - ECG: QRS Wave Detection, P Wave and T Wave Detection, Dicrotic Notch Detection; Heart Sound Detection; EEG Rhythm Detection
Frequency Domain Characterisation of Biomedical Signals - Power Spectrum Density Estimation
Wave Shape and Waveform Complexity Analysis - Normal and Ectopic Beat, Arrhythmia Analysis, Analysis of Exercise ECG, and Analysis of Respiration
